entries.d: you have ten of them on your hands
entries.s: She wore a silver ring on her _____, just above her hand.

entries.d: you hear with it
entries.s: The music is too loud. I cover my _____ with my hands to protect my hearing.

entries.d: you smell with it
entries.s: The clown has a big red _____ in the middle of his face that he can honk.

entries.d: it grows on your head
entries.s: She has long brown curly _____ down to her shoulders.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with HAIR?

entries.d: you see with it
entries.s: She has one blue _____ and one green one, and she uses them to see colours.

entries.d: you eat and talk with it
entries.s: Open your _____ wide and say 'aah' so the doctor can see your throat.

entries.d: hair on a man's chin and cheeks
entries.s: My grandpa has a long grey _____ on his chin because he never shaves.

entries.d: the arm on the same side as your right hand
entries.s: He broke his _____ last week and has a cast from his shoulder to his hand — the one he writes with.

entries.d: the arm on the same side as your left hand
entries.s: She wears her silver watch on her _____, on the side opposite from her writing hand.

entries.d: the body part between your neck and arm
entries.s: The parrot sat on the pirate's _____ just next to his neck and under his ear.

entries.d: the body part you walk and stand with
entries.s: He hurt his _____ in football practice and cannot run today, but he can still walk slowly.

entries.d: the body part at the end of your leg
entries.s: I have a brown shoe on my left _____. The other shoe is missing.

entries.d: the plural of foot
entries.s: My _____ are freezing cold. I need to put on warm woolly socks and slippers.

entries.d: a hard white thing in your mouth
entries.s: My baby sister just got her first white _____ in her lower gum. It is small and sharp.

entries.d: the plural of tooth, in your mouth
entries.s: Brush your _____ every morning and evening with a toothbrush and toothpaste.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with TEETH?

entries.d: high, not short, for people
entries.s: The basketball player is very _____ — about two metres from his feet to his head.

entries.d: not long or not tall
entries.s: She has _____ hair that only reaches her ears, because she cut it last week.

entries.d: too, as well
entries.s: Tom can sing very well, and he can _____ play the guitar at the same time.

entries.d: known by many people
entries.s: This pop singer is very _____. Millions of people know her name and her songs.

entries.d: a word for a boy or man (object)
entries.s: Tom is my best friend. I like _____ very much because he is kind to everyone.

entries.d: belonging to a boy or man
entries.s: That big brown dog is _____. The boy over there loves his pet very much.

entries.d: a big boat on the sea
entries.s: The big _____ with white sails sailed across the deep blue sea to America.

entries.d: having a lot of power in your body
entries.s: He is very _____ and can lift the heavy box with both hands above his head.

entries.d: the leader of a ship
entries.s: The _____ of the ship told all the sailors to turn the boat to the left toward the island.

entries.d: to own or to have something
entries.s: I _____ two older sisters and one baby brother in my family.

entries.d: nice to look at
entries.s: What a _____ pink summer dress you are wearing! I love the flower pattern.

entries.d: a colour, a mix of red and blue
entries.s: She painted the flowers _____ — the colour you get when you mix red and blue.

entries.d: seven days
entries.s: There are seven days — Monday to Sunday — in one _____.

entries.d: one more time
entries.s: I did not hear you clearly. Please say it _____, one more time.

entries.d: to the place where you were before
entries.s: School is over for the day. Let's go _____ home to eat dinner.

entries.d: to make a picture with colours
entries.s: I like to _____ big pictures of animals with my brush and colours.

entries.d: a girl who has the same parents as you
entries.s: My older _____ is twelve years old and she is a girl. We share a bedroom.

entries.d: a young male person
entries.s: That _____ over there in the blue T-shirt is my best male friend Tom from class 1A.

entries.d: a young female person
entries.s: That _____ over there with the red hair in pigtails is called Anna. She is in my class.

entries.d: an adult male person
entries.s: The _____ in the blue shirt with short hair and a tie is our male teacher Mr. Smith.

entries.d: an adult female person
entries.s: The _____ with the long brown hair is my mother. She is about 40 years old.

entries.d: a phrase telling who a girl or woman is
entries.s: This is my younger sister. _____ Anna and she is 7 years old.

entries.d: a phrase telling who a boy or man is
entries.s: This is my older brother. _____ Tom and he is 12 years old.
